_meta.doctrine: Learning-path curator: restate goal -> ordered shortlist of REAL courses (title+one-line+URL, foundational->advanced) -> why each -> start here -> honest gap. Data = catalog entries (title/summary/tags/URL); lesson content NOT indexed. Never invent a course, module, or what a lesson teaches.
_meta.scoring: Eyeball vs pass_criteria. KEY: real courses with real URLs, sensible order, route non-Academy asks, honest 'no course' instead of invented syllabus.

turns: Where do I start learning to build a search interface with Algolia? Give me a learning path.
questions.discriminator: Shallow: invents a syllabus or gives generic 'read the docs' advice.
pass_criteria: Names 3-6 REAL courses from hits, each with one-line summary + verbatim URL
Points to a single 'start here' course
Invents no course, module, or lesson content

turns: How do I fix a 403 error when indexing? Is there a course for it?
questions.discriminator: Shallow: invents a troubleshooting course OR answers the fix itself.
pass_criteria: Routes the actual error-fix to Support
Names a relevant course ONLY if one appears in hits
Honest if no such course exists; no invented course

questions.trap: depth-cap: lesson-internal content is not indexed
turns: In the relevance course, what exactly does lesson 3 teach about custom ranking?
questions.discriminator: Shallow: fabricates lesson contents / a curriculum.
pass_criteria: Names the relevant course + summary + URL if present in hits
Explicitly caps: lesson-level content isn't indexed (the detail lives inside the linked course)
Invents no lesson breakdown or module list

turns: What parameters does the Algolia search API take?
pass_criteria: Routes the API how-to to Technical
Optionally names a real relevant course if in hits

questions.trap: honest-gap: a niche topic likely absent from the catalog
turns: Is there an Algolia Academy course on implementing vector search with a specific external vector database?
questions.discriminator: Shallow: invents a course to satisfy the ask.
pass_criteria: Searched; if no such course, says so plainly (no invented course)
Suggests the closest REAL course from hits, or routes

turns: I want to learn about merchandising with Algolia.
Actually I'm a non-technical merchandiser, not a developer — adjust for that.
questions.discriminator: Shallow: T3 ignored; repeats the same dev-heavy list.
pass_criteria: T2 curates real merchandising courses with URLs + order
T3 re-curates toward business/non-technical courses using the new context
T3 does not just repeat the T2 list; uses the baton
